text.title: File Size Guidelines and AI-Agent Gotchas
text.summary: Enforced size budgets and common LLM mistakes that collapse the Rails decomposition convention.

section.title: File size budgets
section.reference: Structural lint script enforcing these budgets in CI

antipattern.description: Pattern collapse: agent puts logic in the controller when asked to "add an endpoint quickly".
antipattern.reason: Lock the convention in CONTRIBUTING.md and load it as system-prompt context for every agent run. Without enforcement, agents regress to fat controllers within one sprint.

antipattern.description: Service returns void: def call; @user.save!; end.
antipattern.reason: Downstream queue jobs cannot compose. Always return the canonical entity or a Result monad.
Whitelist allowed namespaces (services/, queries/, forms/, policies/, serializers/) and block PRs introducing new ones without an ADR.

antipattern.description: Service calling another service in the same request chain.
antipattern.reason: Acceptable if explicit and documented; not acceptable if a deep chain spans transactions. Force agents to declare orchestration in the service docblock.

antipattern.description: Policy bypass via User.find(id) instead of policy_scope(User).find(id).
antipattern.reason: Multi-tenant data leaks via timing attack on find. Use policy_scope at the controller boundary.
